title: Nylas API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Nylas API credential detected in variable assignment. Nylas provides email, calendar, and contacts APIs. Exposing client secrets, API keys, or access tokens allows unauthorized access to email data, calendar events, contacts, and message sending capabilities. This can lead to data breaches, privacy violations, and unauthorized email operations. Store credentials securely using environment variables or secret management systems.

Nylas bearer token detected in authorization header. Bearer tokens provide direct API access to Nylas email, calendar, and contacts services. Exposing these tokens allows unauthorized users to read emails, access calendar events, retrieve contacts, send messages, and perform actions on behalf of connected accounts. Store tokens securely and rotate immediately if exposed.

Nylas Basic authentication detected. Basic authentication headers containing base64-encoded credentials (client_id:client_secret) expose Nylas OAuth credentials. These allow unauthorized OAuth flows, token generation, and access to connected email accounts. Use secure credential storage and never hardcode authentication headers.

Nylas API URL with embedded credential detected. API URLs containing access tokens or client secrets in query parameters expose credentials in logs, browser history, referrer headers, and network monitoring tools. This provides attackers with direct access to email data, calendars, and contacts. Use Authorization headers instead of URL parameters for authentication.

Generic long authentication token detected in Nylas context. Long alphanumeric tokens (40-80 characters) in Nylas-related code often represent access tokens, client secrets, or API keys for email, calendar, and contacts APIs. Exposing these allows unauthorized access to sensitive communication data and user information. Verify token type and rotate immediately.
